title: cassia nictitans var. aspera
intro: Cassia nictitans var. aspera, also known as the American senna or wild senna, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and laxative effects. The plant's seeds and roots are rich in anthraquinone glycosides, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as relieving constipation, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. Cassia nictitans var. aspera can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder for use in herbal remedies, and is often used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
